info.title: Headway API
info.description: Serves computed transit metrics with full lineage, the DQ resolution workflow, and the audited certification action. This API never computes a reported figure; it serves what the calculation library produced, joined to its provenance. Reported values are JSON strings (exact NUMERIC, never float). Auth: local accounts (ADR-0011); the native OIDC relying party is the next increment behind the same {sub, username, role} claim set.

schema.title: Response List Metric Values Metrics Values Get

get.description:
"Explain this number": the full provenance tree from the reported
figure down to the raw records that produced it (ADR-0007).

Dual credential (handoff 0006 follow-up): a signed-in human session OR a
``read:metrics`` machine key. The machine path is rate-limited per key
and audited with actor ``key:<key_prefix>``; the human path is unchanged.

schema.title: Response List Issues Dq Issues Get
Response List Keys Machine Keys Get

get.description:
Computed values for machine consumers (scope ``read:metrics``): same
filters and shape as the human ``GET /metrics/values``. Each row's
lineage is available from ``GET /metrics/values/{metric_value_id}/
lineage`` with this same key.

schema.title: Response Machine List Metric Values Machine Metrics Get

get.description:
Every agency policy setting, with its plain-language description and
who last changed it. Any signed-in role may read — policy must be
visible to the people it governs.

put.description:
Change one policy setting (certifying official only). The old and new
values land together in the audit trail; the change and its audit event
commit in one transaction.

get.description: Serve the MR-20 preview package (NOT reportable) for one month.

parameters.name: month

schema.description: Calendar month as YYYY-MM (e.g. 2026-07), UTC, half-open period.
schema.title: Month

parameters.description: Calendar month as YYYY-MM (e.g. 2026-07), UTC, half-open period.

200.description: The headway_calc.mr20 package for the month, verbatim: form/generator/period header, reportable=false + banner, programmatically enumerated caveats, and the four MR-20 data points per mode plus fleet totals, each cell carrying full provenance or an explicit null + reason.

schema.title: Response List Certified Values Public Metrics Certified Get

get.description:
Serve the agency logo. UNAUTHENTICATED by design — the app shell and
public pages show it before sign-in; the only per-caller control is the
same per-IP token bucket as the public open-data endpoint. The logo is
the least sensitive object in the system: a public image the agency
chose to publish.

200.description: The agency logo bytes, with its stored content type.

404.description: No logo has been uploaded yet.

post.description:
Upload the agency logo (certifying official only — the same role that
owns every other branding setting). SVG or PNG, at most 512 KiB. The
bytes go to the object store BEFORE the settings row and audit event
commit together — the store-before-record ordering of ingest.

get.description:
The branding bundle for the app shell: display name, the two brand
colors (contrast-guaranteed at write time), and whether a logo exists.
UNAUTHENTICATED by design, per-IP rate limited — the shell brands itself
before sign-in, and nothing here is sensitive.

post.description:
Enter one safety event; the classifier runs synchronously and the
entry, its classification, and the audit record commit together.

schema.title: Response List Events Safety Events Get

post.description:
Append-only correction: enter the CORRECTED event in full; the
original stays untouched except for its superseded_by link.

parameters.name: month

schema.description: Month for the S&S-50 deadline rows (YYYY-MM). Defaults to the current UTC month. S&S-40 deadlines are month-independent (every open major event is listed).
schema.title: Month

parameters.description: Month for the S&S-50 deadline rows (YYYY-MM). Defaults to the current UTC month. S&S-40 deadlines are month-independent (every open major event is listed).

get.description:
The plan wizard's vocabulary, straight from the calc selector's
constants (Table 41.01 / §41.07), with the eligibility guidance.

Look up one ready-to-use table cell (verbatim, cited) without
creating a plan — the wizard's live preview. All encoded cells are
readable here, including the reference-only grouped-APTL cells.

post.description:
Create a sampling plan. The required sizes are the calc selector's
verbatim table cells; the selector version is recorded on the row.

schema.title: Response List Plans Sampling Plans Get
Response List Draws Sampling Plans  Plan Id  Draws Get

post.description:
One random-selection act for one period: seeded, recorded, WITHOUT
replacement (§63.03), drawn by the versioned calc drawer.

Record one ride-check observation for one drawn unit. Append-only:
corrections supersede.

schema.title: Response List Measurements Sampling Plans  Plan Id  Measurements Get

post.description:
Append-only correction: enter the CORRECTED observation in full; the
original stays untouched except for its superseded_by link (the
migration-0017 supersede pattern; migration 0020's trigger enforces
it).

get.description:
Measured vs required, per draw and overall, with the worksheet of
drawn-but-unmeasured units.

post.description:
The §83 APTL estimate over the plan's active measurements. REFUSES
Base-option plans and undersampled plans; the result is a SAMPLED
ESTIMATE (provenance-labeled) and is never written to
computed.metric_values.

BrandingResponse.title: BrandingResponse
BrandingResponse.description:
What the app shell needs to brand itself. Colors here have already
passed the WCAG AA contrast guardrail at write time.

source_label.title: Source Label
source_label.description: The envelope source this key is bound to (required for ingest keys); e.g. 'tides_simulated' for a simulator key.

period_label.title: Period Label
period_label.description: Which period this draw covers at the plan's frequency (for example 2026-Q1, 2026-01, or 2026-W14). One draw per period.

service_units.title: Service Units
service_units.description: ALL service units you expect to operate in this period, in the plan's unit of sampling (§63.07) — for scheduled service including trippers, shuttles, and other special operations. Use period-qualified ids (for example 2026-01-15/trip-5012) so a unit id never repeats across periods.

seed.title: Seed
seed.description: Random seed for the draw. Leave blank to let Headway generate one from a cryptographic randomness source. Either way the seed is RECORDED on the draw so the selection can be reproduced and audited.

oversample_units.title: Oversample Units
oversample_units.description: Extra units to select BEYOND the plan's required per-period size. Oversampling is allowed only when the extra units are selected randomly — Headway draws them from the same seeded random order, and flags them on the draw record.

annual_upt_100pct.title: Annual Upt 100Pct
annual_upt_100pct.description: Your agency's 100% count of annual UPT for this mode and type of service — the §83.01(a) expansion factor, as a decimal string.

upt_100pct_by_day_type.title: Upt 100Pct By Day Type
upt_100pct_by_day_type.description: Optional: 100% UPT counts by type of service day (Weekday / Saturday / Sunday) to also get per-day-type estimates (§83.01(b)). Every measurement must then carry its service-day type.

KeyListItem.title: KeyListItem
KeyListItem.description: Listing shape: prefix + metadata only — never the hash, never the key.

LineageNode.title: LineageNode
LineageNode.description:
One node of the provenance tree.

``transform_name``/``transform_version`` describe the transform that
PRODUCED this node from its ``inputs`` (per lineage.edges). Raw records
are leaves: no transform, no inputs.

unit_id.title: Unit Id
unit_id.description: The drawn service unit this ride check observed.

observed_upt.title: Observed Upt
observed_upt.description: Unlinked passenger trips (boardings) counted on this unit by the ride checker.

observed_pmt.title: Observed Pmt
observed_pmt.description: Passenger miles traveled measured on this unit, as a decimal string (values are exact decimals, never floats).

service_day_type.title: Service Day Type
service_day_type.description: Weekday, Saturday, or Sunday — required later only if you want estimates broken out by type of service day.

service_date.title: Service Date
service_date.description: The date the ride check was performed.

unit_id.title: Unit Id
unit_id.description: The drawn service unit this ride check observed.

observed_upt.title: Observed Upt
observed_upt.description: Unlinked passenger trips (boardings) counted on this unit by the ride checker.

observed_pmt.title: Observed Pmt
observed_pmt.description: Passenger miles traveled measured on this unit, as a decimal string (values are exact decimals, never floats).

service_day_type.title: Service Day Type
service_day_type.description: Weekday, Saturday, or Sunday — required later only if you want estimates broken out by type of service day.

service_date.title: Service Date
service_date.description: The date the ride check was performed.

reason.title: Reason
reason.description: Why the original observation is being corrected (kept in the audit log — the original measurement itself is never edited).

report_year.title: Report Year
report_year.description: The NTD report year this plan samples for.

mode.title: Mode
mode.description: NTD mode code the ready-to-use plans cover: DR (demand response), VP (commuter vanpool), MB/TB (bus), CR (commuter rail), LR/HR/MR/AG (other rail).

type_of_service.title: Type Of Service
type_of_service.description: Type of service this plan samples (e.g. DO for directly operated, PT for purchased transportation). Sampling meets the FTA floor per mode AND type of service.

unit.title: Unit
unit.description: Unit of sampling and measurement (Table 41.01): vehicle_days, one_way_trips, round_trips, one_way_car_trips, or one_way_train_trips — must match the mode.

efficiency_option.title: Efficiency Option
efficiency_option.description: 'aptl' (report a 100% UPT count, sample average passenger trip length; for bus this is the Without Route Grouping column) or 'base' (sample both UPT and PMT; note that Base-option ESTIMATION is not yet available — Section 70).

frequency.title: Frequency
frequency.description: Sampling frequency: quarterly, monthly, or weekly.

PublicMetricValue.title: PublicMetricValue
PublicMetricValue.description: A certified figure, publishable form. No PII, no certifier identity.

occurred_at.title: Occurred At
occurred_at.description: When the event happened (include the time zone).

mode.title: Mode
mode.description: The ONE mode this event is reported in. If more than one mode was involved, apply the Predominant Use Rule (p. 15): rail wins over non-rail; otherwise pick the mode carrying more passengers.

type_of_service.title: Type Of Service
type_of_service.description: Type of service (e.g. DO for directly operated, PT for purchased transportation).

narrative.title: Narrative
narrative.description: What happened, in your own words.

fatalities.title: Fatalities
fatalities.description: How many people died (confirmed within 30 days; suicides are included — Exhibit 5, p. 16). Do NOT count deaths from illness, overdose, or natural causes (p. 20); DO count a death of undetermined cause in a rail right-of-way that may be the result of collision or electrocution (p. 20).

injuries.title: Injuries
injuries.description: How many people were taken directly from the scene for medical care (Exhibit 5, p. 16 — immediate transport, not later self-referral). Do NOT count transport solely for illness, natural causes, exposure, intoxication, overdose, or an unrelated mental-health evaluation, or declarations of self-harm with no evident injury (p. 22); DO count an injury the event caused, e.g. a passenger's heart attack caused by a collision (p. 22).

property_damage_usd.title: Property Damage Usd
property_damage_usd.description: Estimated property damage in dollars. Sum the damage to ALL vehicles and property involved or affected — transit and non-transit — plus the cost of clearing wreckage (p. 25; e.g. a $15,000 car plus $12,000 bus damage is reported as $27,000, Example 7A). Leave blank if not yet assessed — Headway never treats unknown damage as $0.

serious_injury.title: Serious Injury
serious_injury.description: Rail only: did anyone suffer a serious injury (p. 21) — hospitalization for more than 48 hours within 7 days; a fracture of any bone (except simple fractures of fingers, toes, or nose); severe hemorrhages, or nerve, muscle, or tendon damage; an internal organ; or 2nd/3rd-degree burns or burns over 5% of the body surface? Answer yes even if the person was NOT transported from the scene — serious injuries 'may or may not have been transported' (p. 21).

substantial_damage.title: Substantial Damage
substantial_damage.description: Rail: did the damage disrupt operations AND require towing, rescue (e.g. a rescue train), on-site maintenance, or immediate removal before safe operation (p. 25)? Do NOT count cracked windows; dents, bends, or small punctures; broken lights or mirrors; or removal under the vehicle's own power for minor repair, testing, or recorder download (p. 25). For cyber events: did the intrusion disrupt operations (Scenario G, p. 19)?

towed.title: Towed
towed.description: Was any vehicle (transit or not) towed away from the scene? For a non-rail collision involving a transit revenue vehicle this alone makes the event reportable (p. 17); for a rail collision, a tow-away IS substantial damage (Example 7C, p. 27).

evacuation_life_safety.title: Evacuation Life Safety
evacuation_life_safety.description: Were people evacuated from a transit facility or vehicle for life-safety reasons (p. 17)?

assault_on_worker.title: Assault On Worker
assault_on_worker.description: Was a transit worker assaulted? An injury is NOT required for this to be reportable on the S&S-50 (p. 3).

grade_crossing.title: Grade Crossing
grade_crossing.description: Rail: did the collision occur at a rail grade crossing or intersection (p. 17)?

runaway_train.title: Runaway Train
runaway_train.description: Rail: did a revenue vehicle move uncommanded, uncontrolled, or unmanned — operator incapacitated, sleeping, or absent, or an electrical, mechanical, or software failure — on the mainline, in a yard, or in a shop (p. 17)?

evacuation_to_rail_row.title: Evacuation To Rail Row
evacuation_to_rail_row.description: Rail: did people evacuate to the controlled rail right-of-way (transit-directed or self-evacuation)? Evacuation to a platform does not count unless it was for life safety (p. 17).

SafetyEventCreate.title: SafetyEventCreate
SafetyEventCreate.description:
One safety event as entered by agency staff. Field descriptions are
plain language — the entry form asks questions, not jargon.

SubscriptionResponse.title: SubscriptionResponse
SubscriptionResponse.description: Subscription shape served back — deliberately secret-free.

occurred_at.title: Occurred At
occurred_at.description: When the event happened (include the time zone).

mode.title: Mode

reason.title: Reason
reason.description: Why the original entry is being corrected (kept in the audit log — the original event itself is never edited).
